refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall optimize each phase of the load→travel→dump cycle to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-functions aligned with the cycle phases enables targeted design parameters and clearer verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for each sub-phase (load, travel, dump) under representative duty cycles and site conditions.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize load phase cycle time
FR2: Optimize travel phase cycle time
FR3: Optimize dump phase cycle time

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive cycle phase scheduling with dynamic resource allocation
DP2: High-performance vehicle drivetrain and control system
DP3: Real-time teleoperation support for cycle phase acceleration
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive cycle phase scheduling with dynamic resource allocation

potential_sources: Interdependence between cycle phases requiring coordinated scheduling
Data exchange between scheduling logic and vehicle control
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically encapsulates cycle time optimization by managing phase transitions and resource allocation, minimizing cross-phase coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle and teleoperation modules can respond to scheduling commands in real time
Risk that dynamic scheduling complexity may introduce latency impacting cycle time
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor real-time responsiveness of vehicle and teleoperation modules to scheduling commands; consider fallback strategies.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level coordination and scheduling of cycle phases to meet cycle time targets

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Send scheduling commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to optimize cycle phases

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receive real-time status and performance data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for scheduling adjustments

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as scheduling logic is centralized here; low diffusion as interfaces are well-defined.
Vehicle executes commands but does not contain scheduling logic; moderate diffusion due to control feedback.
Teleoperation supports execution but scheduling logic is external; moderate diffusion.
Safety Services not involved in cycle time scheduling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing adaptive scheduling in NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and enables clear ownership of cycle time optimization.
containment_risks: Potential latency in command propagation to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Complexity of real-time scheduling may require tight integration with Vehicle and Teleoperation
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate communication latency and consider partial delegation of scheduling to Vehicle or Teleoperation if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes cycle time KPI into manageable sub-functions; selected DP logically addresses these with adaptive scheduling.

diffusion_risks: Scheduling logic centralized but depends on timely data and command exchange with Vehicle and Teleoperation
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as owner of scheduling DP; interfaces are well-defined and limited.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle times and monitoring interface data flows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of scheduling DP to NGHE Solution IM; Vehicle and Teleoperation act as execution modules.

recommended_revisions: Monitor real-time communication latency between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.
Consider fallback or partial scheduling delegation if latency impacts cycle time targets.

decision.justification: The current FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirements and architectural constraints with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize load phase cycle time
FR2: Optimize travel phase cycle time
FR3: Optimize dump phase cycle time

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can respond in real time to scheduling commands.
Communication latency remains within acceptable bounds.
open_risks: Potential latency impacting cycle time optimization.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of command and telemetry interfaces.
Performance data validating cycle time improvements under duty cycle.
